description: Chromium WebGPU integration overlay. Current browser diagnostics are wrapper evidence only; source-level Doe runtime ownership requires the Chromium runtime selector markers checked by check_chromium_source_checkout.py --require-runtime-selector.

coverage.notes: Observed in browser diagnostics, but not accepted as source-level Doe execution until Chromium owns the runtime selector and fail-closed path.
Observed in browser diagnostics, but the mounted source checkout must expose Doe load/proc-surface/instance failure handling before this can be marked passing.
Diagnostic coverage remains useful, but it is not proof that Chromium's WebGPUDecoderImpl selected Doe.
Smoke coverage exercises WebGPU work through the diagnostic lane; source-level claimability waits on the Chromium selector seam.
Render smoke output remains diagnostic until the browser binary exposes the Doe runtime source markers.
Canvas context evidence does not by itself prove Doe runtime ownership inside Chromium.
Readback behavior stays diagnostic until source-level runtime identity is present.
Copy readback coverage remains diagnostic without Chromium-owned Doe selection.
Diagnostic coverage is retained, but claim status requires the source selector.
Upload coverage is not source-level Doe evidence until the selector marker gate passes.
Queue completion behavior remains diagnostic pending WebGPUDecoderImpl ownership.
Doe-side implementation may exist in native lanes, but Chromium source still needs selector and wire/proc interception before browser evidence can pass.
Browser-level external texture evidence waits on source-level selector and instance/proc-surface handling.
Resource lifecycle evidence remains diagnostic without source-level Doe runtime identity.
Timestamp query coverage is retained as diagnostic browser evidence.
Native implementation exists, but this path is not exercised by current Chromium browser diagnostics.
Native implementation exists, but render bundle browser evidence still requires source-level Doe selection.

wireProtocolNotes.architecture: Current source checkout evidence does not show Chromium-owned DoeCommandDecoder or WebGPUDecoderImpl runtime selection markers. Browser smoke artifacts remain diagnostic wrapper evidence until --require-runtime-selector passes.
wireProtocolNotes.instanceLifetime: Doe instance/proc-surface failure and teardown behavior must be handled in Chromium source before forced-Doe rows can claim source-level runtime identity.
wireProtocolNotes.externalTextureGap: External texture browser evidence remains blocked until source-level selector and wire/proc interception land in Chromium.
